source.title: Polymerase-guided base editing enables in vivo mutagenesis and rapid protein engineering

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: Existing in vivo mutagenesis systems (e.g., CRISPR-X, EvolvR) have limited mutation windows (~40 bp), require multiple gRNAs to tile a gene, inhibit mutation at gRNA binding sites, and have fixed mutation biases. The goal is to develop a targeted, continual, inducible in vivo mutagenesis platform with high mutation rates, broad mutation windows, tunable mutation spectra, and minimal off-target effects, inspired by B-cell somatic hypermutation.

section_gists.heading: Polymerase-guided base editing enables in vivo mutagenesis and rapid protein engineering
section_gists.gist: Introduces TRIDENT, a platform for targeted in vivo mutagenesis using T7 RNAP fusions to deaminases and DNA repair factors. Achieves mutation rates >10^-3 s.p.b., >1000-fold on-target specificity, and tunable diversity at all four nucleotides. Addresses limitations of prior systems (CRISPR-based, TRACE) such as narrow windows, fixed biases, and off-target effects.
process_signals: Prior systems limited by low mutation rates, narrow windows, fixed biases, and off-target effects.
TRIDENT designed to mimic B-cell SHM by targeting mutagenic enzymes via T7 RNAP.
Core components: cytosine deaminase (PmCDA1), engineered adenosine deaminase (yeTadA1.0), and DNA repair factors.
methods: Fusion of PmCDA1 to T7 RNAP for targeted cytosine deamination.
Use of T7 promoter to recruit fusion protein to target locus.
Incorporation of DNA repair factors (e.g., Apn2p, Msh6p) to enhance A/T/G mutagenesis.
Engineering of adenosine deaminase (yeTadA1.0) for A>G editing.
failures_or_limits: TRACE lacks chemically inducible tunability and has low diversity (biased to C/G transitions).
Moore et al. and Álvarez et al. systems had significant off-target mutation frequency.
TRIDENT still shows bias towards transition mutations.

section_gists.heading: Results
section_gists.gist: Demonstrates stepwise development: (1) PmCDA1-T7 RNAP fusion increases mutation rate at target with T7 promoter; (2) optimization reduces off-target effects via genomic integration and inducible promoter, achieving 1800-fold on:off-target ratio; (3) addition of DNA repair factors (Apn2p, Msh6p) increases A/T and G mutations; (4) engineered adenosine deaminase (yeTadA1.0) enables A>G editing; (5) applications: reversion of URA3 stop codon, evolution of red-shifted mCherry, and pyrimethamine-resistant PfDHFR.
process_signals: Initial PmCDA1-T7 RNAP fusion gave high on-target mutation but also 2500-fold off-target increase.
Optimization via linker length, DNA binding domains, and expression control improved specificity.
Genomic integration and inducible promoter (β-estradiol) yielded best on:off-target ratio (1800-fold).
DNA repair factors Apn2p and Msh6p increased A/T mutations 5-fold and G mutations ≥5-fold.
Engineered yeTadA1.0 increased A/T mutation rate 270-fold.
TRIDENT evolved mCherry red-shift (F23S) and PfDHFR pyrimethamine resistance (D54N) rapidly.
methods: Fluctuation assay (URA3 LOF on 5-FOA) to measure mutation rates.
NGS to characterize mutation spectra and windows.
Serial selection with increasing pyrimethamine for PfDHFR evolution.
metrics: On-target mutation rate: up to 1.1×10^-2 LOF mutations/division (initial), tunable 2.5×10^-5 to 2.7×10^-3 (optimized).
Mutation window: ~2 kb downstream of T7 promoter.
A/T mutation rate increased 5-fold with repair factors; 270-fold with yeTadA1.0.
mCherry red-shift achieved in 1 day; PfDHFR resistance in 11 days (5 rounds).
failures_or_limits: Overexpression or targeting of UNG reduced overall mutation rates (contrary to mammalian systems).
Mutation spectrum still biased toward transitions.
Single T7 terminator may not fully prevent downstream mutagenesis.

section_gists.heading: Discussion
section_gists.gist: Summarizes TRIDENT's development: mimics SHM, uses T7 RNAP for targeting, combines deaminases and repair factors. Achieves high, tunable mutation rates and base diversity. Demonstrates rapid protein engineering (mCherry, PfDHFR). Acknowledges bias toward transitions and suggests further tuning of repair pathways. Notes need for multiple terminators to prevent downstream mutagenesis.
process_signals: TRIDENT built to mimic polymerase-based targeting of SHM.
PmCDA1-T7 RNAP alone gives >99% C>T transitions in UNG-deficient yeast.
Co-targeting Apn2p and Msh6p increased A/T and G mutations.
yeTadA1.0 balanced A/T and C/G mutagenesis.
TRIDENT generates ~1 mutation/kb/16 h growth.
Evolved mCherry red-shift (F23S, T>C) and PfDHFR resistance (D54N, G>A) despite transition bias.
Robustness: 177/180 replicates grew in 3 mM pyrimethamine.
metrics: PfDHFR evolution: 11 days (5 rounds), faster than prior systems.
failures_or_limits: Bias toward transition mutations; transversions limited.
Single T7 terminator may allow downstream mutagenesis; Moore et al. suggest ≥4 terminators.
Further tuning of DNA repair pathways needed to expand mutation spectrum.

section_gists.heading: Methods
section_gists.gist: Describes experimental procedures: strain construction (CEN.PK2-1Dα, W303α), plasmid construction (Gibson assembly, gap repair), fluctuation assays for URA3 LOF/GOF, NGS library prep (Nextera XT), and base editor evolution (error-prone PCR of TadA, selection for URA3 reversion).
process_signals: Fluctuation assays used to measure mutation rates (Falcor algorithm).
NGS used to characterize mutation spectra and windows.
Base editor evolution involved error-prone PCR and selection for URA3 stop codon reversion.
methods: Fluctuation assay: URA3 LOF on 5-FOA, URA3 GOF on -ura plates.
NGS: Bowtie alignment, quality filtering (Q>35), frequency calculation.
Base editor evolution: error-prone PCR with mutagenic dNTPs, selection in uracil-lacking media.

section_gists.heading: Next-gen sequencing and analysis for characterization of TRIDENT system.
section_gists.gist: Details NGS workflow: growth with inducer, genomic DNA extraction, PCR of target region, Nextera library prep, HiSeq/MiSeq sequencing. Data analysis: Bowtie alignment, quality filtering (Q>35), base frequency calculation, moving average with outlier removal (3-sigma).
process_signals: NGS used to quantify mutation frequencies and spectra.
Outlier removal to reduce N-mer sequence artifacts.
methods: Moving average over 21 bp window, excluding outliers >3 SD from mean.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: Fusing a cytidine deaminase to T7 RNAP will target mutations to a T7 promoter-defined locus, mimicking SHM.
Adding DNA repair factors (Apn2p, Msh6p) will increase A/T and G mutation rates by diverting repair to error-prone pathways.
An engineered adenosine deaminase (yeTadA1.0) can balance A/T and C/G mutation rates.
Tunable expression and genomic integration will reduce off-target effects.
The combined system (TRIDENT) will enable rapid protein engineering via high, diverse mutation rates.
uncertainties_to_resolve: Can transversion rates be increased by further engineering repair pathways?
What is the optimal number of T7 terminators to prevent downstream mutagenesis?
How does TRIDENT perform in other organisms or chromosomal contexts?
What are the long-term genomic stability and off-target effects over many generations?

events.summary: CRISPR-based targeting systems have narrow mutation windows (~40 bp), inhibit mutation at gRNA binding sites, and have fixed mutation bias.
TRACE lacks chemically inducible tunability and has low diversity biased to C/G transitions; Moore et al. and Álvarez et al. systems have significant off-target mutation frequency.
TRIDENT is designed to mimic B-cell somatic hypermutation by targeting mutagenic enzymes via T7 RNAP, using cytosine deaminase, engineered adenosine deaminase, and DNA repair factors.
Plasmid-based expression of PmCDA1-T7 RNAP increased off-target mutagenesis by more than 2500-fold above background, resulting in a net on:off-target ratio of only 60-fold.

events.summary: TRIDENT with PmCDA1 achieves precise initiation of mutation and constant sustained mutation rate across a target region.
numbers_or_metrics: first increase in mutation rate at cytosines 9–12 bases from RNA transcription initiation site

events.summary: Expanded TRIDENT platform to target DNA-repair factors involved in A/T/G mutagenesis in SHM to enhance mutational diversity.
Overall mutation spectrum remained biased as over 99% of induced mutations are C>T and G>A substitutions.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk describes characterization of TRIDENT mutation window and spectrum, expansion to target DNA repair factors, and initial results showing increased A/T and G mutagenesis but persistent transition bias.

events.summary: Designed directed evolution and selection to engineer adenosine deaminase with increased A/T mutations when fused to T7 RNAP.
Performed five rounds of evolution and selection, observing increased TAA-reverting colonies.
NGS results showed enhanced A/T mutagenesis in YHM5 and YHM6, with mutation frequency below 5e-4 at any single base in P T7.
Confirmed TRIDENT strains YHM5 and YHM6 retained similar on/off-target mutation rates to optimized YHM2, with minimal fitness impact.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk describes engineering of adenosine deaminase for TRIDENT, including hypothesis, directed evolution, validation, and integration into advanced strains.

events.summary: TRIDENT applied to correct a TAA stop codon in mCherry via FACS, yielding 0.14% mCherry-positive cells after one sort.
TRIDENT generates mutations at all four nucleotides on a plasmid-based mCherry with T7 promoter targeting.
TRIDENT evolved a red-shifted mCherry variant (F23S) with a 5 nm shift in emission maximum.
TRIDENT used to evolve pyrimethamine resistance in PfDHFR; 177/180 replicates grew to saturation after 5 passages.
Sequencing of pyrimethamine-resistant replicates identified D54N as the most common mutation, present in >85% of sequences.
Validation confirmed D54N significantly improves yeast growth rate in pyrimethamine; other mutations not statistically significant.
TRIDENT shows bias towards transition mutations; transversions limited.
Single T7 terminator may not fully prevent downstream mutagenesis; Moore et al. suggest ≥4 terminators.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers application of TRIDENT to mCherry stop codon reversion, red-shift evolution, and PfDHFR pyrimethamine resistance evolution, plus discussion of limitations.
This chunk describes detailed experimental methods for fluctuation assays, NGS analysis, base editor evolution, mCherry screening, and PfDHFR evolution, which are core to the paper's process.

events.summary: PacBio sequencing of PfDHFR evolution experiment yielded 2.3 million raw reads, filtered to 486,000 usable reads.
Four significant coding mutations were identified and validated for growth impact by expressing PfDHFR variants in yeast with 3 mM pyrimethamine.
Growth rates of PfDHFR variants were measured in triplicate over 36 h, with statistical significance determined by t-test.
High variability in TAA mutation rates with SHL-fusion strains led to increasing N to 24 for that experiment.
Yeast continuous evolution experiments were carried out in a highly parallel format over weeks, with growth results replicated by cloning individual sequences.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk primarily describes validation and reproducibility details for the PfDHFR evolution experiment, including sequencing, mutation significance criteria, growth assays, and handling of variability. It does not introduce new hypotheses or major design changes.

evidence_bank.quote: mutations are limited to an approximately 40 bp window adjacent to the gRNA target site, mutation of the 20 bp gRNA binding site is inhibited, and mutagenesis is focused to just two or three base pairs

effective diversification of a 1000 bp gene can require more than 20 gRNAs, requiring redesign for each new target and inhibiting mutation at over 400 bp of sequence corresponding to gRNA binding sites

Moore et al. and Álvarez et al. also developed a cytidine deaminase T7 RNA Polymerase targeting system in E. coli, but did not utilize DNA-modifiers to increase mutation diversity and both systems, while more targeted than chemical mutagenesis, still retained significant off-target mutation frequency

TRIDENT consists of up to three components targeted to a locus of interest via T7 RNA polymerase: (1) a cytosine deaminase enzyme that introduces C>T and G>A substitutions, (2) an engineered adenosine deaminase enzyme that introduces A>G and T>C substitutions, and (3) DNA-repair factors that further enhance A/T/G mutational diversity
TRIDENT takes inspiration from the highly targeted mutagenesis that occurs in B-cell somatic hypermutation (SHM)
Plasmid-based expression of PmCDA1-T7 RNAP increased off-target mutagenesis by more than 2500-fold above the background rate, resulting in a net on:off-target ratio of 60-fold
TRIDENT with PmCDA1 achieves several important characteristics of engineered in vivo mutagenesis, including precise initiation of mutation and constant sustained mutation rate across a target region.
the first increase in mutation rate occurring at cytosines that are 9–12 bases from the RNA transcription initiation site of T7 RNAP
We expanded the TRIDENT platform to target DNA-repair factors involved in A/T/G mutagenesis in SHM to enhance mutational diversity.
We co-localized yeast homologs of twelve DNA-repair factors involved in SHM A/T/G mutagenesis to the T7 RNAP fusion using modular SHL/SH3 domains
the overall mutation spectrum remained biased as over 99% of induced mutations are C>T and G>A substitutions
We used directed evolution and selection to engineer an adenosine deaminase enzyme that exhibited increased A/T targeted mutations when fused to T7 RNAP (Fig. 3a).
We observed a substantial increase in the number of TAA reverting colonies on media lacking uracil after five rounds of evolution (Supplementary Fig. 12).
The results demonstrate that YHM5 and YHM6 exhibited enhanced A/T mutagenesis compared to YHM2 and YHM4 as a result of the localization of the engineered yeTadA1.0 deaminase by T7 RNAP (Fig. 3c).

We also confirmed that the most advanced TRIDENT strains, YHM5 and YHM6, retained similar on and off-target mutation rates to YHM2, which had been optimized for lower off-target mutagenesis (Supplementary Fig. 13).
There was no statistically significant difference (p < 0.05) in growth rate for any level of β-estradiol induction up to 100 nM (Supplementary Table 3).

the D54 residue is within 3 Å of the pocket where pyrimethamine binds

event_graph.paper_main_problem: Existing in vivo mutagenesis systems have limited mutation windows, require multiple gRNAs, inhibit mutation at binding sites, and have fixed mutation biases. The goal is to develop a targeted, continual, inducible in vivo mutagenesis platform with high mutation rates, broad windows, tunable spectra, and minimal off-target effects.

canonical_events.summary: CRISPR-based targeting systems have narrow mutation windows (~40 bp), inhibit mutation at gRNA binding sites, and have fixed mutation bias.
TRACE lacks chemically inducible tunability and has low diversity biased to C/G transitions; Moore et al. and Álvarez et al. systems have significant off-target mutation frequency.

TRIDENT is designed to mimic B-cell somatic hypermutation by targeting mutagenic enzymes via T7 RNAP, using cytosine deaminase (PmCDA1), engineered adenosine deaminase (yeTadA1.0), and DNA repair factors (Apn2p, Msh6p).
Plasmid-based expression of PmCDA1-T7 RNAP increased off-target mutagenesis by more than 2500-fold above background, resulting in a net on:off-target ratio of only 60-fold.
Optimization via genomic integration and inducible promoter (β-estradiol) achieved up to 1800-fold on:off-target ratio and tunable mutation rate over 100-fold range.
TRIDENT with PmCDA1 achieves precise initiation of mutation and constant sustained mutation rate across a target region, with mutation window up to 2 kb.
key_numbers: first increase at cytosines 9–12 bases from initiation site

canonical_events.summary: Overall mutation spectrum remained biased as over 99% of induced mutations are C>T and G>A substitutions.
Expanded TRIDENT platform to target DNA-repair factors (Apn2p, Msh6p) involved in A/T/G mutagenesis in SHM to enhance mutational diversity.
Designed directed evolution and selection to engineer adenosine deaminase (yeTadA1.0) with increased A/T mutations when fused to T7 RNAP.
Performed five rounds of evolution and selection, observing increased TAA-reverting colonies; engineered yeTadA1.0 increased A/T mutation rate 270-fold.
Confirmed TRIDENT strains YHM5 and YHM6 retained similar on/off-target mutation rates to optimized YHM2, with minimal fitness impact.
TRIDENT applied to correct a TAA stop codon in mCherry via FACS, yielding 0.14% mCherry-positive cells after one sort.
TRIDENT evolved a red-shifted mCherry variant (F23S) with a 5 nm shift in emission maximum.
TRIDENT used to evolve pyrimethamine resistance in PfDHFR; 177/180 replicates grew to saturation after 5 passages (11 days).
Sequencing of pyrimethamine-resistant replicates identified D54N as the most common mutation, present in >85% of sequences.
Validation confirmed D54N significantly improves yeast growth rate in pyrimethamine; other mutations not statistically significant.
TRIDENT shows bias towards transition mutations; transversions limited.
Single T7 terminator may not fully prevent downstream mutagenesis; Moore et al. suggest ≥4 terminators.

edges.reason: Both describe limitations of existing systems, with K02 focusing on TRACE and off-target issues.
Limitations motivate the design of TRIDENT.
Initial implementation of TRIDENT design leads to high off-target mutagenesis.
Failure prompts optimization to reduce off-target effects.
Optimized system allows characterization of mutation window and rate.
Characterization reveals bias in mutation spectrum.
Bias motivates expansion to target DNA-repair factors for diversity.
Validation confirms engineered strains retain low off-target rates.
Validated strains used for mCherry stop codon reversion.
Stop codon reversion leads to evolution of red-shifted variant.
mCherry evolution demonstrates protein engineering capability, followed by PfDHFR evolution.
Sequencing analysis identifies key resistance mutation.
Validation confirms functional impact of D54N.
Despite success, limitation of transition bias is noted.
Additional limitation regarding T7 terminator is discussed.

must_cover_points: Characterization of mutation window and rate
mCherry stop codon reversion and red-shift evolution
Identification and validation of D54N mutation
Limitations: transition bias and T7 terminator
